# Security Policy

## Scope

This is a small **public template / playground** repository. It is not a production service and should not hold customer data or live credentials.

## Supported Versions

Only the latest commit on `main` is maintained.

## Reporting a Vulnerability

Open a **public GitHub issue** to report security concerns, unless you believe disclosure would cause active harm—in that case contact the maintainers directly.

When reporting, include:

- A description of the issue
- The file(s) affected
- Any suggested fix if you have one

## What Should Never Be Committed

- Credentials, tokens, API keys, or passwords of any kind
- Private keys or session cookies
- Large exports of personal data

If you spot any of the above committed by mistake, open an issue immediately.
